assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a foreign currency relative to at least one group entity's functional currency.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies forecast transactions, including certain highly probable intragroup foreign-currency transactions in consolidated financial statements, as a distinct hedged-item model.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets or liabilities, including qualifying monetary items, as a distinct hedged-item model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedges of foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
